example_project.name: VibeKB
example_project.note: This is VibeKB's own living software model — VibeKB explaining VibeKB. The source it describes is THIS repository (cubixmeow-commits/VibeKB). Bundled example models of other applications live under examples/ (see examples/sousmeow/.vibekb) and are NOT the active model; do not confuse them with the current state of VibeKB.

provenance.name: VibeKB
provenance.verification_scope: The content loader, dynamic guide, static generator, validator, topology test, and self-maintenance CLI were traced from source in this repository. Diagram topology (architecture, content-load-flow) is verified-from-source. Homepage layout verified by rendering index.php (section order problem → install → compatibility → understanding → proof). Homepage install commands verified against INSTALLER.md, README.md, go.mod (Go 1.24+), and internal/installer argument parsing: clone → go build -o vibekb ./cmd/vibekb → ./vibekb install [options] [target]; dry-run uses ./vibekb install --dry-run; installation does not require PHP; PHP 8.2+ is required after install for the guide and model commands. Coming soon badges list only Phase 2 distribution items (prebuilt binaries, Homebrew, Winget, curl) and no longer claim Native CLI or Repository doctor. Homepage Compatibility & Requirements claims checked against PRODUCT.md, INSTALLER.md, DEPLOYMENT.md, and ARCHITECTURE.md. Homepage copy-button click behaviour is inferred-from-source (Clipboard path reviewed; not clicked in a browser here). docs/ labelled as generated via php tools/vibekb.php generate, not installed. The interactive functionality map (overview first screen) was verified previously by rendering the overview in both modes and driving the map in a headless browser. Installation is native to the Go binary (embed.go + internal/installer): verified previously end-to-end with PHP removed from PATH; tools/lib/Starter.php reads template/starter/; install.php is a compatibility wrapper (see decision:native-installer-embedded-payload, change:native-go-installer, change:homepage-native-installer-copy). Homepage voice pass (change:homepage-voice-pass): prose-only rewrite verified by diffing index.php so install command variables and rendered install/dry-run strings match the prior native-installer homepage commit; layout/CSS unchanged; php -l index.php clean. Phase 2a release binaries (change:release-binaries-pipeline): .github/workflows/release.yml cross-compiles six platforms with CGO_ENABLED=0, -trimpath, -ldflags -s -w and Version/Commit/Built injection; checksums.txt uploaded; vibekb version output verified locally with ldflags; homepage/docs primary path is GitHub Releases download (no Go required to install); PHP 8.2+ still post-install. Workflow not yet exercised by a real v0.1.0 tag in this environment.
provenance.freshness_note: VibeKB does not update this model on its own. An AI agent keeps it synchronized while developing VibeKB, following the workflow in CLAUDE.md. `source_commit` records the commit the model was last reconciled against; run `php tools/vibekb.php check` to see what has changed since — drift is detected mechanically, but interpreting a change into the model still requires an agent.

structure.project: project/ — identity, intent, current state, constraints
structure.system: system/ — mental model, components, flows, storage, deployment
structure.memory: memory/ — decisions, constraints, assumptions, warnings, discoveries, changes
structure.work: work/ — current AI work, handoff, sessions
